reasoning: Since no resolution dates were explicitly listed, I am assuming a single probability for the forecast due date (2026-01-24). The reference value of 30-day average fatalities in Bangladesh relies on a period that includes the very high-fatality protests in July/August 2024. Therefore, the 30-day average (currently ~31.17) is substantially elevated compared to typical baseline periods. In a typical 30-day window in early 2026, fatalities are less likely to exceed this elevated baseline unless there is another major spike in violence (e.g., related to elections or sudden regime instability). Since Bangladesh recently underwent a massive regime change and is currently under an interim government, stability may gradually improve, making a ~31 fatality rate in 30 days relatively unlikely. Thus, I estimate a 45% probability.
